description_long: This traffic barricade consists of a horizontal bar supported by two A-frame legs, with a distinctive yellow and black diagonal striped pattern across the main panel. The structure appears to be made primarily of metal, likely steel or aluminum, with the striped panel possibly being a painted or laminated metal or plastic sheet. The legs are angled outward for stability and have flat feet to rest securely on pavement or other hard surfaces. The barricade is lightweight and portable, designed to be easily moved and positioned to block or redirect pedestrian or vehicular traffic. The surface finish is matte, with the stripes serving as a high-visibility warning. There are no visible lights, signage, or additional fixtures attached to the barricade.
description: A portable metal traffic barricade with yellow and black diagonal stripes, supported by two A-frame legs. Designed for high visibility and easy placement in urban environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full striped panel and both A-frame support legs clearly.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the barricade and the angled support legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the striped panel and the structure of the legs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the angled legs and the thin profile of the barricade.
attribute_reflective_striping_pattern: Diagonal yellow and black stripes, alternating, medium width, covering the main panel.
